changes.description:
Two real bugs fixed: (1) setUser → setUserData (wrong callback identifier — would have caused TS2304 once the parser error was resolved); (2) React component was truncated — missing return statement and closing brace. Added 'return
{user?.displayName}
; };' to properly close the component. Block now passes as .tsx.
Missing trailing comma after 'displayName: John Doe' before 'authToken' in client.login() object literal. TS1005 ',' expected. Added comma.
Same missing comma issue in the session handler example block (TypeScript tab). Added comma after 'displayName: John Doe'.

get_user_information_54_66.block_summary: React FC component showing UserRepository.getUser() via useEffect hook
bugs: setUser used as callback — undeclared (setUserData is the correct variable from useState)
Component function body truncated — missing return statement and closing brace
get_user_information_54_66.action: Fixed both: corrected setUser→setUserData, added return/closing brace to complete the component

authentication_198_207.block_summary: client.login() basic example with userId, displayName, authToken
authentication_198_207.bug: Missing trailing comma after displayName in the object literal
authentication_198_207.action: Added comma — JavaScript/TypeScript object literals require commas between properties

authentication_599_622.block_summary: Production session handler example with token renewal, then client.login() call
authentication_599_622.bug: Same missing comma after displayName in the nested client.login() call

notes.observation: All 3 were genuine doc bugs that a developer copy-pasting would hit immediately. The doc-as-test framework caught real copy-paste errors, confirming its value beyond the regex validator.
